title: Pentacalia sailapatensis
taxonomy: Pentacalia sailapatensis is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristic flower structure, which resembles that of other Asteraceae species. Pentacalia sailapatensis is further classified within the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The genus Pentacalia is part of the tribe Eupatorieae, which is a subfamily within the Asteraceae family. Pentacalia sailapatensis is a relatively rare species, and further research may be needed to confirm its classification.

intro: Pentacalia sailapatensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the Pentacalia sailapatensis or Andean Pentacalia. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Pentacalia sailapatensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional medicine, Pentacalia sailapatensis is often used to make herbal infusions and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into ointments and tinctures to leverage its therapeutic properties.
